meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Version Control: Advanced Student Guide
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and fix version control issues. An advanced case study for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: To solve the instability caused by lack of version control while implementing SaaS pricing, you must treat your pricing logic as immutable infrastructure. By utilizing Antigravity to scaffold your billing state and Claude Code to automate versioned migrations, you ensure your revenue logic remains robust, auditable, and production-ready.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first SaaS, but every deployment feels like a game of Russian roulette. Without version control, a single tweak to your pricing logic could bleed revenue or lock out your users, leaving you with nothing but a broken codebase and a sinking feeling in your gut.

But the gap between a student project and a professional SaaS is just a few architectural choices away. By integrating rigorous versioning and professional-grade pricing tiers, you aren't just writing code—you're building a business. Let’s evolve your stack from 'experimental' to 'enterprise-ready' using the latest AI-assisted workflows.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Governance
steps.description: Establish a base state for your project to ensure that every pricing iteration can be rolled back if a calculation error occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust directory structure for a SaaS application that separates core business logic from billing modules, ensuring all pricing configurations are stored in versioned JSON files.

steps.title: Define Immutable Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Hardcoding prices is a rookie mistake. We define tiers as schema-validated objects that can be versioned alongside your app code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript schema for multi-tier SaaS pricing including monthly/annual billing cycles and feature flag mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create pricing-schema.ts using the generated schema'

steps.title: Automate Versioned Migrations
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write migration scripts that ensure existing user subscriptions aren't orphaned when you update pricing models.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a migration script that handles the transition of user subscription objects when pricing tier IDs are updated in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate migration-001-pricing-update.ts with rollback capability'

steps.title: Integrate Webhook Event Handling
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to Stripe webhooks, ensuring the system state always matches the payment provider's version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an event handler for Stripe webhooks that validates subscription changes against our local versioned pricing schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add webhook-handler.ts for stripe subscription.updated events'

steps.title: Final QA and Version Audit
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure no unversioned code paths exist in your billing pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security and versioning audit on the current pricing implementation, identifying potential race conditions in subscription state updates.

pro_tips: Always use UUIDs for pricing plans; never rely on database auto-increment IDs which break during multi-environment deployments.
Implement a 'dry-run' flag in your billing migrations to simulate production state changes without modifying live customer records.
Treat your pricing configuration as code (PaC) and keep it in a dedicated repository to decouple billing changes from UI deployments.
Use feature flags to roll out new pricing tiers to a subset of users before a full global release to mitigate revenue impact.
Maintain a 'dead-letter queue' for failed webhook events to ensure that payment synchronization errors can be replayed after a fix.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel or Railway, run a full integration test using your new migration scripts, and push your first versioned release to production.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Professionalize your workflow, secure your revenue logic, and launch with the confidence of a lead engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
